rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for binding assignments.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define interface behavior using visible ports and features.
Implementation contracts should bind to subcomponents and connections using valid implementation scope identifiers.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure AGREE expressions align with the actual AADL data types of ports and features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference visible component ports and features.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-specific bindings in component type annexes.
Do not reference AADL property names directly as AGREE variables without model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
